# Recommended Architecture: Anelise Methodology MVP

**Generated:** 02/03/2026
**Generated By:** @architect (Aria)
**Story:** WIS-15
**Status:** Ready for Team Review & Approval

---

## Executive Recommendation

### System Design: Pragmatic Monolith + Modular Services

**Summary:** Start with a **unified application** (monolith with clear service boundaries) to hit the 16-day timeline. Design it for **easy extraction to microservices** later, but don't over-engineer now.

**Why?**
- ✅ Fast to build (no service communication overhead)
- ✅ Single database is simpler for tight timeline
- ✅ Easier debugging and deployment
- ✅ Can extract services later when volume demands it

**Risk Mitigated:** Won't paint us into architectural corners—clear module boundaries mean services extract cleanly in Phase 2 (post-MVP).

---

## Technology Stack Recommendation

### Frontend
```yaml
Framework: Next.js 15 (React)
  - Reason: Full-stack capability (can serve API routes if needed)
  - Built-in: SSG/SSR, Image optimization, API routes
  - Best for: Forms + collaborative notebook + landing page

Styling: Tailwind CSS
  - Reason: Fast iteration, utility-first
  - Bundle impact: Minimal with Next.js

Form Library: React Hook Form
  - Reason: Lightweight, excellent for 28-question form

Collaborative Editor: Lexical or TipTap
  - Reason: Notebook needs real-time collaboration feel
  - Fallback: Simple textarea if timeline pressured

State Management: Zustand or Context API
  - Reason: Lightweight, no boilerplate
  - Note: Avoid Redux (overkill for MVP)

Charts/Dashboard: Recharts
  - Reason: Simple, good defaults for progress visualization
```

### Backend
```yaml
Runtime: Node.js 20 LTS + TypeScript
  - Reason: Fast development, same language as frontend
  - Type safety: Critical for complex scoring logic

Framework: Fastify (or Express if team familiar)
  - Reason: Fastify is faster, smaller learning curve than Nest.js
  - Alternative: Express if team prefers simplicity
  - Avoid: Nest.js (too heavy for MVP timeline)

API Style: REST with JSON (or Supabase RPC functions)
  - Reason: Simple, all teams understand REST
  - Supabase RPC: Can use PostgreSQL functions for complex logic (optional)
  - GraphQL: Defer to Phase 2 (if needed)
  - tRPC: Consider only if team already using React + Node

Database Client: Supabase JS Client
  - import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
  - Type-safe queries with auto-inference
  - RLS enforced at database level (not code level)

Service Layer Pattern: Feature-based modules (not models-based)
  - scoring/        (uses Supabase queries + RPC for 4D algorithm)
  - form/           (saves form_responses, enforces RLS)
  - approval/       (reads scores, writes approvals)
  - calendar/       (schedules sessions)
  - email/          (transactional via Resend)
  - etc.
```

### Database
```yaml
Primary: Supabase (PostgreSQL 15+ managed)
  - Reason: Built-in Auth, RLS policies, real-time capable
  - Features: Row Level Security for client data isolation
  - Dashboard: Built-in SQL editor for quick queries
  - Extensions: UUID, pgcrypto already enabled

Query: Supabase JS Client or raw SQL
  - Supabase JS Client: Type-safe queries, auto-parsing
  - Raw SQL: For complex queries via supabase.rpc() or direct SQL
  - ORM Option: Prisma with Supabase adapter (if team prefers abstraction)
  - Avoid: Custom JDBC/connections (Supabase client is optimized)

Migrations: Supabase migration files (SQL)
  - Reason: Version controlled, reversible, story-aligned
  - Location: `supabase/migrations/{timestamp}_*.sql`
  - Apply: Via Supabase CLI (`supabase db push`)

Authentication: Supabase Auth
  - Reason: OAuth + Email built-in, JWT tokens
  - RLS: Policies tied to auth.uid()
  - Sessions: Managed by Supabase (no custom session table needed)
```

### External Services (Managed)
```yaml
Email: Resend (or SendGrid)
  - Reason: Simple API, React email component support (Resend)
  - Use case: Transactional + batch emails
  - Cost: ~$0.001/email (negligible at MVP scale)

Authentication: Supabase Auth (built-in)
  - Reason: OAuth/email out-of-box, JWT tokens, RLS integration
  - Database: Sessions managed by Supabase (no custom table)
  - Avoid: NextAuth.js (Supabase Auth is native + simpler)

File Storage: AWS S3 (or Vercel Blob)
  - Reason: PDFs, notebook exports, attachments
  - For MVP: Vercel Blob is simpler (if hosting on Vercel)

Calendar Integration: Google Calendar API
  - Reason: Industry standard, clients already use it
  - Alternative: Calendly API for simpler booking

PDF Generation: PDFKit (Node) or Puppeteer
  - Reason: PDFKit is lightweight, Puppeteer for HTML→PDF
  - Use PDFKit for scorecard + blueprint (text-heavy)
  - Use Puppeteer for complex layouts (fallback)
```

### DevOps & Deployment
```yaml
Frontend Hosting: Vercel (Next.js)
  - Reason: Next.js native, zero-config deployment
  - Environment: Node.js 20 LTS
  - Build: `npm run build` (Next.js)

Backend Hosting: Vercel Edge Functions (or Railway.app)
  - Reason: Fastify serverless-compatible
  - Alternative: Railway.app for simplicity (traditional Node.js)
  - Env vars: SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (from Supabase project)

Database Hosting: Supabase (included)
  - Reason: Managed PostgreSQL + Auth + RLS
  - Backups: Automatic daily
  - Connection pooling: Supabase PgBouncer built-in

CI/CD: GitHub Actions
  - Reason: Free tier sufficient for MVP
  - Triggers: Push to main → tests → deploy
  - Supabase migrations: Run `supabase db push` before deploy

Monitoring: Vercel Analytics (built-in) + Supabase logs + Sentry for errors
  - Reason: Supabase has built-in query monitoring
```

### Testing
```yaml
Unit Tests: Vitest
  - Reason: Faster than Jest, great for TypeScript
  - Focus: Scoring algorithm, validation logic
  - Target: >70% coverage on critical paths

Integration Tests: Vitest + supertest
  - Reason: Test API endpoints + database interactions

E2E Tests: Playwright
  - Reason: Fast, good debugging experience
  - Scope: Critical user paths (form → approval → booking)
  - Target: 5-10 key scenarios for MVP

Linting: ESLint + Prettier
  - Reason: Standard setup, enforces consistency
```

---

## Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        CLIENT LAYER                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ · Landing Page (marketing, form entry)                          │
│ · Diagnosis Form (28 questions, multi-step)                     │
│ · Client Portal (status, notebook access)                       │
│ · Intelligent Notebook (collaborative editor)                   │
│ · 90D Dashboard (progress tracking)                             │
│ (All built with Next.js + React + Tailwind)                     │
└────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │ HTTPS / JSON
┌────────────────▼────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      API LAYER (Fastify)                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Authentication Module (NextAuth.js integration)         │   │
│  │ · Session management                                    │   │
│  │ · OAuth + Email auth                                    │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ EPIC 1 Services (Diagnosis Infrastructure)              │   │
│  │ · form-router: Validates & saves form responses         │   │
│  │ · scoring-service: 4D algorithm + business logic        │   │
│  │ · email-service: Send score + diagnosis hypothesis      │   │
│  │ · pdf-service: Generate diagnosis PDF                   │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ EPIC 2 Services (Approval Process)                      │   │
│  │ · approval-router: Approval/rejection logic             │   │
│  │ · calendar-service: Google Calendar integration         │   │
│  │ · email-service: Send confirmations                     │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ EPIC 3 Services (Diagnosis Validation)                  │   │
│  │ · notebook-service: Store & retrieve notebook data      │   │
│  │ · blueprint-service: Generate 7D action plan PDF        │   │
│  │ · email-service: Send blueprint + upsell               │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ EPIC 4 Services (90D Tracking)                          │   │
│  │ · checkin-service: Weekly check-in submission           │   │
│  │ · feedback-service: Generate + send feedback            │   │
│  │ · reporting-service: ROI calculation & PDF              │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Shared Infrastructure Services                          │   │
│  │ · email-queue: Async email processing (Bull/BullMQ)    │   │
│  │ · file-storage: S3 or Blob API integration              │   │
│  │ · logging: Structured logging (Pino)                    │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
└────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                 │ SQL / Prisma ORM
┌────────────────▼────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   DATA LAYER (PostgreSQL)                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Tables:                                                         │
│ · users (id, email, auth, metadata)                             │
│ · form_responses (id, user_id, answers_json, scored_at)        │
│ · scores (id, user_id, dimension_1-4, total, created_at)       │
│ · approvals (id, user_id, status, reason, created_at)          │
│ · sessions (id, client_id, status, scheduled_at)               │
│ · notebooks (id, session_id, content_json, updated_at)         │
│ · blueprints (id, session_id, pdf_url, created_at)             │
│ · checkins (id, user_id, week, answers_json, created_at)       │
│ · emails_sent (id, user_id, type, sent_at, status)             │
│                                                                 │
│ Migrations: Prisma (version controlled, reversible)             │
│ Indices: On frequently queried columns (user_id, created_at)    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Database Schema (Story 1.1 + Aligned)

Based on Story 1.1 requirements + EPIC 1-4 needs:

```sql
-- Clientes (clients) — Story 1.1 requirement
CREATE TABLE clientes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  nome VARCHAR(255),
  status VARCHAR(50), -- 'prospect', 'enrolled', 'rejeitado', 'completado'
  data_inscricao TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  user_id UUID, -- Link to Supabase auth.users (optional, for RLS)
  CONSTRAINT fk_user FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Questões (questions) — Story 1.1 requirement (28 pre-loaded)
CREATE TABLE questoes (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  numero INT CHECK(numero BETWEEN 1 AND 28), -- Question number 1-28
  dimensao VARCHAR(50), -- 'Desalinhamento', 'Ruído', 'Vazamento', 'Maturidade'
  texto TEXT NOT NULL, -- Question text
  tipo VARCHAR(50), -- 'rating' (1-5), 'text', 'choice'
  ativa BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
  UNIQUE(numero)
);

-- Respostas (responses) — Story 1.1 requirement
CREATE TABLE respostas (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  questao_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES questoes(id),
  valor_resposta INT, -- 1-5 for ratings
  data TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(cliente_id, questao_id) -- One response per question per client
);

-- Scores (scores) — Story 1.1 requirement
CREATE TABLE scores (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  desalinhamento INT, -- Dimension 1: 1-5
  ruido INT, -- Dimension 2: 1-5
  vazamento INT, -- Dimension 3: 1-5
  maturidade INT, -- Dimension 4: 1-5
  total INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (desalinhamento + ruido + vazamento + maturidade) STORED,
  data TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(cliente_id)
);

-- Decisões (decisions) — Story 1.1 requirement
CREATE TABLE decisoes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  score_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scores(id),
  hipotese_preliminar TEXT, -- Preliminary hypothesis
  aprovado BOOLEAN, -- approval decision
  data_analise TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(cliente_id)
);

-- Aprovações (approvals) — EPIC 2.1 extension
CREATE TABLE aprovacoes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status VARCHAR(50), -- 'aprovado', 'rejeitado', 'pendente'
  motivo TEXT,
  data TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(cliente_id)
);

-- Sessões (sessions) — EPIC 2.2, 3 extension
CREATE TABLE sessoes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  agendado_em TIMESTAMP,
  completado_em TIMESTAMP,
  notas TEXT,
  status VARCHAR(50), -- 'agendada', 'completada', 'cancelada'
  criada_em TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Cadernos (notebooks) — EPIC 3.2 extension
CREATE TABLE cadernos (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  sessao_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sessoes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  conteudo JSONB, -- Rich text/blocks
  link_compartilhado UUID,
  atualizado_em TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Blueprints (blueprints) — EPIC 3.3 extension
CREATE TABLE blueprints (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  sessao_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sessoes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  url_pdf VARCHAR(2048),
  acoes_planejadas JSONB, -- 7D action items
  criada_em TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE(sessao_id)
);

-- Check-ins (checkins) — EPIC 4.2 extension
CREATE TABLE checkins (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  numero_semana INT,
  respostas JSONB, -- Weekly check-in answers
  submetido_em TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Emails Enviados (emails_sent) — Audit trail for all EPICs
CREATE TABLE emails_enviados (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  cliente_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES clientes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  tipo_email VARCHAR(50), -- 'score_hipotese', 'aprovacao', 'confirmacao_sessao', 'blueprint', 'lembrancao_checkin', 'feedback', 'relatorio_roi'
  destinatario VARCHAR(255),
  enviado_em TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  status VARCHAR(50), -- 'enviado', 'falha', 'rejeitado'
  id_externo VARCHAR(255) -- Resend message ID
);

-- Indices
CREATE INDEX idx_clientes_email ON clientes(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_clientes_status ON clientes(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_questoes_dimensao ON questoes(dimensao);
CREATE INDEX idx_respostas_cliente ON respostas(cliente_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_respostas_questao ON respostas(questao_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_scores_cliente ON scores(cliente_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_decisoes_cliente ON decisoes(cliente_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_aprovacoes_cliente ON aprovacoes(cliente_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessoes_cliente ON sessoes(cliente_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_cadernos_sessao ON cadernos(sessao_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_blueprints_sessao ON blueprints(sessao_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_checkins_cliente ON checkins(cliente_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_checkins_semana ON checkins(cliente_id, numero_semana);
CREATE INDEX idx_emails_enviados_cliente ON emails_enviados(cliente_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_emails_enviados_tipo ON emails_enviados(tipo_email);

-- RLS Policies (Row Level Security)
-- Enable RLS on all tables
ALTER TABLE clientes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE respostas ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE scores ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE decisoes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE aprovacoes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE sessoes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE cadernos ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE blueprints ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE checkins ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Clients see only their own data
CREATE POLICY "Clientes veem só seus dados" ON clientes
  FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid()::uuid = user_id);

CREATE POLICY "Clientes veem suas respostas" ON respostas
  FOR SELECT USING (
    cliente_id IN (SELECT id FROM clientes WHERE user_id = auth.uid()::uuid)
  );

-- (Similar policies for all other tables...)
-- Admin users can see all (via service role key in backend)
```

**Migration File Location:** `supabase/migrations/{timestamp}_initial.sql` (to be created in Story 1.1)

---

## Service Composition (Monolith with Module Boundaries)

```
genialidade-app/ (monorepo)
├── packages/
│   ├── backend/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── modules/
│   │   │   │   ├── form/           # EPIC 1.2
│   │   │   │   ├── scoring/        # EPIC 1.3
│   │   │   │   ├── email/          # EPIC 1.4, 2.3, 3.4, 4.3
│   │   │   │   ├── approval/       # EPIC 2.1
│   │   │   │   ├── calendar/       # EPIC 2.2
│   │   │   │   ├── notebook/       # EPIC 3.2
│   │   │   │   ├── blueprint/      # EPIC 3.3
│   │   │   │   ├── checkin/        # EPIC 4.2
│   │   │   │   ├── feedback/       # EPIC 4.3
│   │   │   │   └── reporting/      # EPIC 4.5
│   │   │   ├── shared/
│   │   │   │   ├── auth.ts         # Authentication
│   │   │   │   ├── db.ts           # Prisma client
│   │   │   │   ├── email-queue.ts  # Bull queue
│   │   │   │   ├── storage.ts      # S3/Blob
│   │   │   │   ├── errors.ts       # Error classes
│   │   │   │   └── types.ts        # Shared types
│   │   │   ├── routes/
│   │   │   │   └── (auto-generated from modules)
│   │   │   ├── middleware/
│   │   │   │   ├── auth.ts
│   │   │   │   ├── errorHandler.ts
│   │   │   │   └── logging.ts
│   │   │   └── server.ts           # Fastify app entry
│   │   ├── __tests__/
│   │   ├── prisma/
│   │   │   ├── schema.prisma
│   │   │   └── migrations/
│   │   ├── tsconfig.json
│   │   └── package.json
│   │
│   └── frontend/
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── app/                # Next.js App Router
│       │   │   ├── (auth)/
│       │   │   │   └── login/
│       │   │   ├── (client)/
│       │   │   │   ├── form/       # EPIC 1.2
│       │   │   │   ├── portal/     # EPIC 1.6
│       │   │   │   ├── notebook/   # EPIC 3.2
│       │   │   │   ├── dashboard/  # EPIC 4.1
│       │   │   │   └── checkin/    # EPIC 4.2
│       │   │   ├── (landing)/
│       │   │   │   └── page.tsx    # EPIC 5.1
│       │   │   └── api/            # API routes (if needed)
│       │   ├── components/
│       │   │   ├── FormComponent.tsx
│       │   │   ├── NotebookEditor.tsx
│       │   │   ├── DashboardCard.tsx
│       │   │   ├── EmailTemplates.tsx (for review)
│       │   │   └── shared/
│       │   ├── hooks/
│       │   │   ├── useForm.ts
│       │   │   ├── useAuth.ts
│       │   │   └── useApi.ts
│       │   ├── lib/
│       │   │   ├── api.ts          # Fetch wrapper
│       │   │   ├── validation.ts
│       │   │   └── utils.ts
│       │   ├── styles/
│       │   │   └── globals.css
│       │   └── types/
│       │       └── index.ts
│       ├── __tests__/
│       ├── tailwind.config.js
│       ├── next.config.js
│       ├── tsconfig.json
│       └── package.json
│
└── root files (shared)
    ├── package.json (workspace)
    ├── turbo.json (monorepo)
    ├── docker-compose.yml (local dev)
    └── README.md
```

---

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 0: Setup (Day 1)
- [ ] Initialize monorepo (Turborepo or pnpm workspaces)
- [ ] Create backend package (Fastify + TypeScript)
- [ ] Create frontend package (Next.js)
- [ ] Set up database (PostgreSQL local + migrations)
- [ ] Configure environment variables (.env.example)
- [ ] Set up CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

### Phase 1: Database + API Foundation (Day 2 - Story 1.1)
- [ ] Create `prisma/schema.prisma` (all tables)
- [ ] Run migrations `prisma migrate dev`
- [ ] Create authentication module (NextAuth.js)
- [ ] Create shared error handlers + logging
- [ ] Create form submission API endpoint (POST /api/form)

### Phase 2: Diagnosis Workflow (Days 2-5, Stories 1.2-1.5)
- [ ] **Story 1.2:** Form component (28 questions) + submission
- [ ] **Story 1.3:** Scoring algorithm implementation
- [ ] **Story 1.4:** Email service integration + send score
- [ ] **Story 1.5:** PDF generation (diagnosis report)

### Phase 3: Approval & Booking (Days 5-6, Stories 2.1-2.3)
- [ ] **Story 2.1:** Approval logic endpoint
- [ ] **Story 2.2:** Google Calendar integration + booking
- [ ] **Story 2.3:** Confirmation email service

### Phase 4: Validation Workflow (Days 2-9, Stories 3.1-3.4)
- [ ] **Story 3.1:** Create session guide document (content)
- [ ] **Story 3.2:** Notebook component + backend storage
- [ ] **Story 3.3:** Blueprint PDF generation
- [ ] **Story 3.4:** Upsell email + automation

### Phase 5: 90D Tracking (Days 9-14, Stories 4.1-4.5)
- [ ] **Story 4.2:** Check-in form component + submission
- [ ] **Story 4.1:** 90D progress dashboard
- [ ] **Story 4.3:** Weekly feedback automation
- [ ] **Story 4.4:** Monthly session scheduling
- [ ] **Story 4.5:** ROI report generation

### Phase 6: Marketing (Days 15-17, Stories 5.1-5.3)
- [ ] **Story 5.1:** Landing page build
- [ ] **Story 5.2:** Email sequence automation
- [ ] **Story 5.3:** Dynamic copy loading

---

## Agent Assignment

### Primary Development Stream (@dev / Dex)
**Responsible for:** All code implementation across all EPICs

| Story | Primary Tasks | Time Est |
|-------|---------------|----------|
| 1.1 | Database schema + migrations + auth setup | 1-2 days |
| 1.2 | Form component + validation + submission | 1 day |
| 1.3 | Scoring algorithm + tests | 1 day |
| 1.4 | Email service integration | 1 day |
| 1.5 | PDF generation (scorecard + diagnosis) | 1 day |
| 1.6 | Client portal + dashboard | 1 day |
| 2.1 | Approval logic + endpoints | 0.5 days |
| 2.2 | Google Calendar integration + booking | 1 day |
| 2.3 | Confirmation email flows | 0.5 days |
| 3.1 | Session guide (documentation) | 0.5 days |
| 3.2 | Notebook editor component + backend | 2 days |
| 3.3 | Blueprint PDF + action items | 1 day |
| 3.4 | Upsell email automation | 0.5 days |
| 4.1 | Dashboard component + progress calc | 1 day |
| 4.2 | Check-in form + submission | 0.5 days |
| 4.3 | Feedback service + automation | 1 day |
| 4.4 | Monthly session scheduling | 0.5 days |
| 4.5 | ROI report generation + PDF | 1 day |
| 5.1 | Landing page build | 1 day |
| 5.2 | Email sequences (automation rules) | 1 day |
| 5.3 | Dynamic copywriting system | 0.5 days |

### Support Roles
- **@architect (Aria):** Tech decisions, architecture reviews, dependency resolution
- **@qa (Quinn):** Test planning, AC validation, QA gate for each story
- **@ux-design-expert (Uma):** UI/UX review for forms, notebook, dashboard, landing
- **@po (Pax):** Story acceptance criteria validation
- **@sm (River):** Dependency tracking, blockers resolution

---

## Key Decisions Made

### 1. Monolith vs Microservices
✅ **Decision:** Monolith (Fastify backend + Next.js frontend)
- **Reason:** 16-day timeline demands speed over architecture perfection
- **Future:** Can extract services cleanly when needed

### 2. Database Design
✅ **Decision:** PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Reason:** Type safety, migrations, excellent DX for timeline
- **JSONB columns:** For flexible fields (form answers, notebook content)

### 3. Email Service
✅ **Decision:** Resend (or SendGrid as fallback)
- **Reason:** Simple API, React email support (Resend)
- **Not DIY:** Custom SMTP would waste 2-3 days

### 4. PDF Generation
✅ **Decision:** PDFKit for structured PDFs (scorecard, diagnosis)
- **Reason:** Fast, lightweight, no browser dependency
- **Fallback:** Puppeteer if complex layouts needed (unlikely)

### 5. Notebook Editor
✅ **Decision:** Lexical (Meta) or TipTap
- **Reason:** Lightweight real-time collaboration ready
- **Fallback:** Simple textarea with markdown if timeline pressured

### 6. Authentication
✅ **Decision:** NextAuth.js (OAuth + email)
- **Reason:** Integrated with Next.js, Prisma adapter built-in
- **Database:** Store sessions in PostgreSQL (no Redis needed for MVP)

### 7. Testing Strategy
✅ **Decision:** Vitest (units) + Playwright (E2E)
- **Reason:** Fast, TypeScript native
- **Target:** >70% on critical paths, 5-10 E2E scenarios

---

## Deployment & Ops

### Local Development
```bash
# Prerequisites
Node.js 20 LTS, pnpm, Supabase CLI

# Setup
pnpm install
cp .env.example .env.local
# Fill .env.local with SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from Supabase project
supabase start # Starts local Supabase instance
supabase db push # Apply migrations locally
pnpm dev # Starts frontend (Next.js) + backend (Fastify)

# Access
http://localhost:3000 (frontend)
http://localhost:3001 (API)
http://localhost:54323 (Supabase local studio)

# Cleanup
supabase stop
```

### Production Deployment
```
Frontend: Vercel (git push auto-deploys)
Backend: Vercel Edge Functions (or Railway.app for traditional)
Database: Supabase (managed PostgreSQL + Auth + RLS)
Email: Resend
File Storage: Vercel Blob (or Supabase Storage)
Monitoring: Vercel Analytics + Supabase Dashboard + Sentry
CI/CD: GitHub Actions (push → tests → supabase db push → deploy to Vercel)
```

### Environment Variables
```env
# Supabase
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://xxxx.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=...
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=... (backend only, secret)

# Email
RESEND_API_KEY=...

# Google Calendar (Story 2.2)
GOOGLE_CALENDAR_ID=...
GOOGLE_CALENDAR_KEY=...

# Logging
LOG_LEVEL=debug (dev) / info (prod)

# Note: Auth tokens & sessions are managed by Supabase
#       No NEXTAUTH_SECRET or NextAuth config needed
```

---

## Risk Mitigation

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|-----------|
| Notebook editor is complex | Medium | High | Use battle-tested library (Lexical/TipTap), not custom |
| PDF generation delays | Low | Medium | Use PDFKit, test early |
| Email service issues | Low | Medium | Use Resend (reliable), test all flows day 1 |
| Calendar integration bugs | Medium | Medium | Google Calendar API well-documented, use SDK |
| Timeline slip on 1.1 (database) | Medium | **CRITICAL** | Assign experienced dev, pre-design schema (done here) |
| Scope creep | High | High | Strictly enforce stories as written, say "NO" to changes |
| Notebook real-time needs | Low | Low | MVP doesn't require real-time, simple async is fine |
